title: alpinia lauterbachii
intro: Alpinia lauterbachii, also known as the Papuan ginger, is a type of perennial plant native to the tropical regions of New Guinea. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Alpinia lauterbachii include its ability to aid in digestion, reduce nausea and vomiting, and exhibit antioxidant properties. In herbal preparations, the plant's rhizome is commonly used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, and it is also used in traditional cooking as a spice.
